issue.title: Use generated request adapter for WP_Http::request safety options

artifacts.role: WPHX-312.67 runner installing compiler-emitted class-wp-http.php request shell
stock Haxe PHP compile anchor for request helper modules used by the generated shell
safety-options candidate manifest with generated-shell and IR feature evidence
ownership manifest marking the boundary as compiler_emitted_original_path_shell
behavior receipt for the safety-options candidate

claims: WPHX-312.67 now consumes the compiler-emitted original-path WP_Http::request shell instead of a copied/runner-patched class-wp-http.php body.
The safety-options helper remains the bounded Haxe-owned branch claim, with helper modules compiled for the generated shell's runtime calls.
All six deterministic request safety/proxy oracle/candidate probes remain behavior-identical.
non_claims: This does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership.
This does not claim live network/proxy behavior, upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity, or installed distribution behavior.
This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership.
